Don't DARE hesitate in buying this gem!This album SINGLE-HANDEDLY redeems the music industry and makes up  for every immature,"sexploitation"shallow"low-brow"meaning-free"teeny bopper"type of album that it has  put out over the last FIFTEEN years!!Alicia should be PROUD of herself!! And YOU should make it a PRIORITY to get yourself a copy of this MASTERPIECE!! If I weren't limited to rating songs to five stars,I would've given this album SIXTY-SEVEN stars!! Truly,I am THAT impressed with"As I Am"!! This album is the ACME of musical creativity!!TRUST me,you just GOTTA get THIS one,because an album this good only comes along ONCE every CENTURY!!
top_rant.title: Alicia is ASTONISHING just as she IS,with this OVERWHELMINGLY impressive creation!!

top_rant.text:
I was never a real `fan' of musicals until I saw Funny Face. Even then, it took a second viewing for it to really sink in. While not as exuberant as Singin' in the Rain or as grandiose as My Fair Lady, it holds a special place in Audrey Hepburn's heart as one of the most enjoyable performances of her career--and it shows. Audrey only had to play herself to portray Jo, the pearl trapped inside the shell of self-doubt. In many ways, this mirrored her real life at the time. Reflecting her desire for something light after the grueling War & Peace of 1955, the film is bursting at the seams with energy throughout.

Sound quality-wise, the recording does leave a little to be desired. Though not pulled directly from the film as some may indicate, but rather the recording sessions that make up the film's soundtrack, it is identical to what you'll hear while watching it. This would be a prime candidate for a remaster.

On to the tracks! If you're reading this review, you have probably seen the film, but I feel like writing a bit for each one as a reminder.

1) Overture: Funny Face/'S Wonderful/Think Pink

Welcome to Funny Face! As the track begins, the listener expects an orchestral arrangement of the highlights; however, Fred Astaire politely interjects. We're treated to a slightly up-tempo and jazzier version of the title track, which melds into a dreamlike choral interpretation of "`S Wonderful". Also included is Kay Thompson's classic "Think Pink," which makes you wonder why it didn't receive a track of its own.

2) How Long Has This Been Going On?

A close tie with Moon River as my favorite vocal performance of all time from Audrey Hepburn, "How Long..." encapsulates the essence of Jo at the beginning of the film. Endearingly melodramatic, it is a very meticulous performance that places importance in every word, their pace, and placement. The orchestral interlude late in the song brings up the memory of Jo's unforgettable scene dancing with herself and the hat left behind in the wreckage of her bookshop.

3) How Long Has This Been Going On? [Instrumental Reprise]

A short postscript accompanies the performance involving a delicate clarinet lead following the melody of the title lyric, floating on a soft backdrop of intermittent strings.

4) Funny Face

Earning its distinction as the lightest and most unassuming number on the record, the title track melds the ballroom elegance and zest of its music with the silly, cheerful spirit of its subject matter: "You fill the air with smiles, for miles and miles and miles." My favorite part of the song is the step-ladder ascent of the strings, climbing up the scales at the climax--perfect!

5) Bonjour, Paris!

My favorite song on the record! "Bonjour, Paris!" is a boisterous, energetic production; in a word, BIG. It starts out with what sounds almost like the beginning to a national anthem, and leads into a snippet from the film--Jo, Dick, and Maggie display a false air of indifference to Paris upon arrival, perhaps to impress one another with their worldliness. Once separated, their true tourist curiosity is revealed. Thus begins a romanticized jaunt into Paris led by our three explorers, starting first with Dick: "I want to step out, down the Champs-Elysées..." Conspicuously (and deliberately) left absent is the Eiffel Tower, recognized by our three protagonists later in the song: "There's something missing, I know; there's still one place I gotta go," whereupon they discover the same "strictly tourist" nature in each other and embrace it. If this song doesn't put a smile on your face, I'm afraid there's little hope that anything will.

6) Clap Yo' Hands

If each of our three stars has a signature performance on the album, "Clap Yo' Hands" is unequivocally Kay Thompson's. A spicy mixture of swing, jazz, and blues, peppered occasionally with scat vocals for good measure, the track is perhaps the most uncharacteristic and unexpected. It fits into the story through Dick and Maggie posing as the flamboyant, country-fried pair from "Talluh-hassee" in order to gain admittance to the empathicalist gathering. In the middle of the song comes one of my favorite moments in the film: after the brash "ringa dem bells" section, the music comes to an immediate halt. What directly follows is a light, lilting melody accompanied by a hilarious synchronized jig by the actors: "Why, we's the two most friendly vibrations you ever seen." Thompson proves herself to be a force to be reckoned with vocally, demonstrated by the surprisingly high note she hits at the end. Another track sure to make the surliest of surlies crack a smile.

7) He Loves and She Loves

The most romantic song on the album, "He Loves and She Loves" is an ethereal, nearly percussion-less expression of Dick's falling in love with Jo, structured around a clever conjugation theme that reminds me all too well of my foreign-language class days. The delicate orchestral interlude, to allow for the dance, sharpens into a climax in a decidedly dissonant way, resulting in one of the albums few flaws. However a single, wistful violin saves the day and tastefully leads the listener to the close of the song.

8) Bonjour, Paris! [Instrumental Reprise]

Ira Gershwin presents us with a snappy jazz-fusion interpretation of "Bonjour, Paris," barely over a minute, and headed up by a marimba and saxophone leads. A light, brush-like snare drum and the occasional piano contribution are the supporting actors. If you didn't know better, you'd swear this was a Mancini piece written for Breakfast at Tiffany's.

9) On How to Be Lovely

"On How to Be Lovely" is the cutest track in the list. Featuring Thompson and Hepburn, it is an ambitious mid-tempo stage number with less emphasis placed on the band as compared to other songs to accentuate the vocal performances. Thompson's character, Maggie, takes on an almost motherly role to Jo (her parents are left a mystery to the viewer), demonstrating how to be "lovely" when she gets her first taste of fame. Maggie sings her teachings to the budding model, which are then repeated by Jo in true pupil fashion. Their two vocals occasionally meet into a pleasant harmony, and the "doo-wee-oo" sections prove to be delightfully charming.

10) Basal Metabolism

The only true instrumental on the album (barring, of course, the short instrumental reprises), "Basal Metabolism" is at first a disjointed exploration of avant-garde jazz. It is loosely based around the melody of "How Long Has This Been Going On," and conjures up images of a dimly lit, cigar smoke-filled gentleman's club. A minute or so into the song, as demonstrated in the film, a bass string is lifted at an exaggerated height and released, which begins a pluck-pluck rhythm, slowly evolving into a high-energy swing-jazz rendering of the song "Funny Face."

11) Let's Kiss and Make Up

Though the placement of this song belies its appearance in the chronology of the film, I feel the decision to place it as essentially the last song is well merited--for some reason, it just feels right that way. As I mentioned before, each of the stars had their signature moment in the soundtrack, and this is without a doubt Fred Astaire's. It shows his sweet nature, both on camera and off, and his incredible dance routine in the film proved to audiences this old favorite still "had it." The song has lovely flowing sections as well as staccato stop-start rhythms that are reflected in his dance steps. Midway into the song, the melody turns to an alarming tone indicative of a Spanish bullfighter, humorously acted out by Astaire.

12) `S Wonderful

And this, folks, is the icing on the cake. A dreamy, feather-light melody reminiscent of "He Loves and She Loves," it is a love song that plays upon the slang of those who speak too fast to let articulation catch up: `S Wonderful, `S Marvelous that you should care for me. As Dick and Jo have their final dance in the chapel yard, the orchestra takes over the last half of the song, culminating in an extravagant finish.

Above all, if you're anything like me, the soundtrack to Funny Face will make you happy, no matter what mood you're in. I can't think of any other recording that has such an effect on me. We need to be reminded every so often not to take life too seriously.
top_rant.title: Funny Face: 'S Paradise

top_rant.text:
OK, here's my take on Ashlee Simpson's debut record "Autobiography"...SOUNDWISE, it's great, just the sound I look for and love (female, hard-edged POWERPOP), but her ARTISTIC CREDIBILITY is NIL, and the fact her album sounds too EASY make things a bit too SUSPICIOUS here...

Why?? The MARKETING by MTV, her dad, and her name, that's why...

Sooo, Ashlee wants to be the opposite of her sister by being a ROCK CHICK?? Well, her look works, the SOUND of her record and her raw, rough-edged, unpolished voice are correct. She claims to have co-written all the songs here, which is necessary. One big PROBLEM and why she comes up short...what is it?? Here goes:

1-- Well, to be a true blue ROCK CHICK, she has to have PAID HER DUES...NOPE!!!

2--To be an AUTHENTIC rocker, she HAS to have slugged it out in clubs for YEARS, be a true blue songwriter (words AND TUNES, words ALONE from a diary or whatnot are not enough and do not count!! At the very least, team up with a co-writer in your band or someone you find ON YOUR OWN), be a MUSICIAN (aka, play guitar, bass, keyboards, drums, or whatelse)...NOPE!!!

3--Have no IMMEDIATE connections in your family hook you up, no matter if he's a manager or knows this person or that person...you have to slug it out and PROVE YOURSELF FIRST!!! Develop your sound on your own terms!!! Ashlee, of course, turns to her dad, who hooks her up with a deal at Geffen/Interscope, and hot producer/songwriter John Shanks. Even though the guy makes great records and writes great songs, Ashlee's connections to all this make things MORE than a little TOO smooth-sailing, no matter how good the resulting record sounds!!! This RUINS ALL ARTISTIC CREDIBILITY, as no one will take her seriously, no matter if the record sounds good!! The result will be 15 minutes of fame and guilty pleasures, NOT A LONGTERM ARTISTIC CAREER!!! At least not one with respect, and a whole lotta laughter from her peers...

I HOPE, for her sake, Ashlee can survive this artistic crisis because the album, "Autobiography", sounds good, even though it's really not that original. I love the sound...her raw, rough voice sounds like a Courtney Love/Joan Jett hybrid, with a bit of cooing Jill Sobule on the softer moments. Not far off from a Veruca Salt record, a harder-edged Michelle Branch, a better hooked Avril Lavigne, lesser-known bands like Lava Baby + Lash, you get the picture. The GONZOcatchy hooks are all over the place, and the tunes are consistent, if a bit TOO consistent, and a bit varied so it's not one big song blur, although it tends to get samey in spots (the problem with many powerpop, pop-punk, and Ramones + Green Day records where ALL the tunes are catchy)...not a bad thing though...fave tunes: "LaLa", "Surrender", the titletrack, "Pieces Of Me", "Better Off", + "Love Makes The World Go Round"...

And right away, because of Ashlee's connections, the TV show, and everything, TOO-cynical people will automatically yell "She sucks!!", "Bubblegum!!", "Not an artist", etc...and in most cases, they would be right, although there's nothing wrong with true bubblegum pop, and it's all great examples of classic, well-crafted catchy pop by expert songwriting teams and producers...see The Monkees, Archies, cartoon pop music from the 60s + 70s, Sid + Marty Krofft, The Banana Splits, Brill Building, 1910 Fruitgum Co, Ohio Express, and more, you know the deal...

Ashlee's record bucks these accusations right away, from the getgo, in at LEAST 3 ways...I analyzed this record inside out, it's actually kinda fascinating really...

1-- The Approach + Vocals!! Firstly, Ashlee's voice is NOT squeaky clean, it's raw, rough-edged and raspy, like a Courtney Love/Joan Jett hybrid, or like the Muffs' Kim Shattuck without her trademark screaming. I highly DOUBT it was cleaned up in the studio through effects or a vocoder, as I'm sure they wanted to maintain some credibility + honesty here. I'm sure her voice is what it is, and from what I read in reviews here, many kids HATE her voice!! They are USED to a clean-sounding voice, not that of a rough-edged femme rocker!! I happen to dig her voice, really!! I'd love to hear Ashlee perform live!!

Secondly, the album is NOT synth-drenched purepop/dancepop!! It's a LOUD, LEAN, HARD-EDGED, rocking POWERPOP record!! I KNOW many kids have not heard this sound before, NOR were they expecting it, but something more familiar, bright and clean and something that they can relate to. NOPE, not here!! The subject matter is kinda dark and edgy. It's still catchy though, just not in the way TRL kids like...as a result, they call it, to quote one review, "VERY VERY VERY BAD". Nope, they just don't understand it. I happen to enjoy it, and I'm a 36 year old guy who lives for records with this kind of sound!! BUT, the songs still sound a bit too EASY, for this kind of approach, kinda weird really.

2-- The Production and Musicianship!! Most all bubblegum pop records involve a HUGE team of over 100 people, many producers, engineers, songwriting teams and musicians. Nope, not here!! On "Autobiography", there is a small, lean team in line. Lessee...how many producers?? ONE, just ONE: the man, John Shanks!! He's worked with great people like Anastacia, Michelle Branch, Sheryl Crow, and many more. Also Hilary Duff, on "Come Clean", but that's OK, great song!! Already bucking the trend. How many engineers/recorders?? ONE!! Jeff Rothschild, that's it!! Songwriters?? Just mainly 1 team of Ashlee Simpson/John Shanks/Kara DioGuardi!!! Unheard of!! Probably here, as Ashlee is clearly NOT an experienced true SONGWRITER, she submitted lyrics or lyrical IDEAS from her diary or something to John, who then hired Kara. John did all the music, clearly, the guy is a TUNESMITH EXTRAORDINAIRE, and he had Kara take Ashlee's "lyrics" and shape them around John's hook-filled melodies. Simple as that, and make them palatable to the point where Ashlee's original root ideas were still there, to retain honesty and so she won't feel like her "songs", aka LYRICS were messed with. Understand?? Very simple. Ashlee gets a credit on every song, when in reality, she had pretty much NOTHING to do with the resulting songs!!! Ashlee is happy (misguided and with delusions of grandeur but happy nonetheless), Geffen is happy, her dad is happy, John and Kara are REALLY happy (they get most of the songwriting royalties!!!). A coupla other songwriters/teams were involved too: Shelly Peiken (1 song), Stan Frazier/Steve Fox/Robbie Nevil/Billy Mann (1 song), and John Feldmann (1 song, he's in a band on Maverick).

Musicians?? Just a core of TWO: Ashlee Simpson (all vocals and backing vocals) + John Shanks (guitars, bass, backing vocals and SOME keyboards...another bucker, most bubblegum pop records involve TRACKS upon TRACKS of keyboards + synths...here, very minimal, if at all!!!)...unheard of in mainstream pop, just rock!! Now, there are a handful of guest musicians, all great, who are from cool bands and who have played on many cool records over time!! They are: Kenny Aronoff (Drums, hot drummer-for-hire, from John Mellencamp's band of many years, plays on 5 songs), Jeff Rothschild (Drums, also drummer-for-hire, plays on 3 songs), Abe Laboriel Jr (Drums, hot drummer-for-hire lately, plays on 3 songs), Kara DioGuardi (backing vocals on 6 songs), Jamie Mehoberac (Piano + Organ, 1 song), Patrick Warren (Chamberlain, 2 songs), David Campbell (String Arrangement, 2 songs)...no bad, and not bubblegum, in sound, just marketing and approach, NOT rock + roll...

In the end, this album is well-crafted and great, loud powerpop, but lost + confused in it's marketing approach!! The marketing is geared towards TRL kids and pre-teen kids, but the SOUND is nothing they would like or really appreciate or "get". Only ADULT rock + rollers, powerpop fans and girlband fans would, BUT they would not take Ashlee or this record seriously or listen with an open mind. Why?? Because of the marketing and the audience it's trying to reach, the TRL kids and pre-teenpoppers!!! Kind of a Catch-22 really!! In the end, this record will sell, cuz of her image, MTV show and hype, BUT most copies will hit the used bins the second day because kids will either HATE it or "not get it". Which is really too bad. I don't see much of a future for Ashlee really, as a result...

I have a feeling "Autobiography" will only be appreciated in 20 or 30 years...when it is uncovered/discovered by curious ears to this then-artifact...I can see it now, "Hey, this is pretty good really!!" Time will be kind to this record, I know it, but that time is just not now.

So basically, Ashlee Simpson's record is a good example of an expertly-crafted pop PRODUCT with loud, rocking powerpop SOUND, out to either FOOL the targeted public (TRL kids) or please them, you be the judge. Well-crafted, absolutely!! Artistic credibility for Ashlee?? Nope!!! My advice for Ashlee?? In the future, no matter how good the record sounds, Ashlee, you had nothing to do with it!!! If you want to be a credible rocker?? DUMP EVERYTHING!!!! Your dad, your record deal, your TV show, your friends, everything!!! Go into hiding for 5 or so years, until people forget about you. Then emerge with an unrecognizable look, change your name, move into a small apartment, learn the music business, form a band ON YOUR OWN, write some REAL songs WITHOUT ANY HELP, and start playing clubs with little fanfare!! Build an HONEST FOLLOWING!!! Get a deal on your own terms with a new lawyer YOU hire...all this BEFORE people discover your past. Look at Alanis Morissette!!! She was in your shoes...follow her lead...

Nuff said...Great record but no credibility for Ashlee...too easy and she had too much help...that's NOT rock + roll!!

Sorry Ashlee, just being honest...
top_rant.title: PRETTY GREAT....BUT her CREDIBILITY IS SHOT....

top_rant.title: Content Information

top_rant.text:
Unlike Peter M. Roche, being an old geezer & prog fanatic I discovered this INCREDIBLE(but SADLY 'somehow' OVERLOOKED save the small cult following made up by the smartest/luckiest U.S.& elsewhere prog heads!)energetic, totally original virtuoso group of Brits!

Being a 53 yr old professional musician; I began my professional career whilst still in HS at AGE 16 & b/c I could grow vg facial hair; ie: a goatee, I played at all types of venues(back when a horse was your ONLY means of transportation;) including clubs that served alcohol & never ONCE was carded or stopped b/c of my young age. I'm so happy/blessed, to still be playing today professionally, & the music I played later was heavily influenced by prog bands. EG's: would be, "GG"-"FZ"-"KC"-"Led Zep"-the MAN who was doing it long BEFORE SRV, "Johnny Winter"-"Savoy Brown"-HENDRIX, & early influences like the INCREDIBLE "JAMES BROWN" & other R&B/Soul/Blues music, "Sir JEFF BECK" & all the way back to "The Ventures"-Dwayne Eddy & SANDY "Papers" NELSON!! Tks to you I do what I DO!:->

But enuff about me, this is about possibly the greatest progressive band that almost no one in the Western Hemisphere ever heard of(!!)& indeed that is a travesty:( This band was so damned good, they certainly 'should have' taken their rightfull position along side the likes of "King Crimson"-"Yes" & "ELP" ect

but unfortunately, SOMEHOW(??)just didn't?? However IF there were a "Mount Rushmore" for the finest prog musicians, the Schulman Brothers(et al)faces would be carved in Granite!!

I MUST tell part of the story that put my bandmates & myself on the same huge stage w/the great "GG", & getting to meet a few members of this seminal band, SO PLS bear w/me. Our band even shared 1 of the several trailers that were set up for the bands to relax in before or after playing with the great guys from "FOGHAT".

In 1973 at the famous/infamous Labor Day weekend Pop Fest called

"Bull Island"(known before the 73 fest, as the "Erie Canal Soda Pop Festival".(Just "Google" it.)which drew an insane crowd of anywhere between 400,000, or AT THE LEAST 250,000 hippies!! I saw the crowd & I firmly believe the higher figure, b/c as i'm sure you've all seen the many documentary films of "Woodstock"(the original)"The Altamont Speedway"-"The Isle of Wight" ect. ect. So by these fests I calculated the crowds #'s & was also tld the higher figure was the closest to the truth by the promoters.

The band I was part of then was called "C. K. Thunder". A POWER TRIO w/a vocalist. This band was what us 'old folk' called a HEAVY ROCK BAND...long before there was Metal music, or this is what led to that; like The WHO-Hendrix-Deep Purple, ect. So being the drummer I was surrounded by STACKS & STACKS of "MARSHALL" Super Lead amps & cabs as well as a WALL of "Acoustic" 360 Bass amps! Enuff to MOVE YOUR KIDNEYS!!:)REALLY.

So WE shared the same huge stage as "GG", & the ONLY way to get on the 25 foot high stage was being lifted by 2 fork lifts, 1 on ea. side of the stage!

We were 'sandwiched' between "FOGHAT"(Already hugely sucessful by then comprised of the ex-"Savoy Brown" members minus Kim Simmonds, w/Rod Price on searing slide guitar. "Foghat" ruled the 'Fest' THAT DAY & the strangest act "I/WE" ever proceded, came in the duo of "Cheech & Chong"!! I have no real knowledge of "C&C's" history but this 'must' have been the LARGEST audience they ever performed their 'stand-up act' before?! We didn't go on until after "Foghat" & it must have been Midnite or after when we hit our 1st chord.

B/c the memory fades at my age, 'I believe' we("CK Thunder")played the 1st nite at "Bull Island" which was/is an island of farmland surrounded by rivers in Indiana, very near the Illinois border. There were ONLY 2 roads in. 1 for a smal amount of vans/smaller 24 foot long trucks & stepvans for those selling mainly food,(such as char cooked, unshucked corn on the cob that was made above long & deep pits in the ground. Believe me it's a delicassy after ANY ice brought in the 1st day had melted so perishables were NOT an option! The OTHER single lane dirt road was for the bands NOT arriving by helicopter(only less than a handful)& their equipment, & of all the musicians/raodies we spoke to ALL had the same thought, that the OLD wooden bridge that took you across the river would collapse under the weight!HA

The other road was for others w/water & people selling the trinkets of that time, leather goods, hippie jewelery & such. THERE WERE NO CONSESSSION STANDS like at the hyper commercialized "Woodstock" Anniversary debacles! So 90% of the audience had to hike a minimum of 4-5 miles to get to the 'proposed site. That didn't work for the 100'd of 1000's the promters 'somehow' didn't expect??!!

Be SURE to Check out webmaster Dan Barret's excellent "GG" web page at:

[...]

for an amazing amount of info including a day by day TOUR HISTORY

of "GG" & so much more on these incredible 'young musicians' in their 'heyday'!!

I'll be emailing Dan next to confirm that, INDEED "GG" played the fest on the late afternoon of the 2nd day, & as 1 member of "GG" recalls(CORRECTLY!! I believe it was Kerry?)on the "Tour Page" for 1973; "THE GIANT(S)" played 1 of their tightest, finest sets at that fest! Kerry said it was the best gig they played in over a month. I know as I wacthed for about 90 mins w/my bandmates, all somewhat awestruck by their virtouosity, unreal LIVE sound, & everyone's ability to play multiple instruments!!

I'll also tell Dan that while SOME of the most famous bands advertised to play the fest didn't but many more did than didn't! B/c "Black Sabbath", REFUSED to helicopter in, even tho their equipment was ALREADY THERE, THIS IS WHAT GOT THE PUBLICITY. "Sabbath", after seeing the tv film footage of the UNBELIEVABLE amount of hippes & FAR too little leased farmland by the promoters, the 80% of non paying audients, just made room where there were crops & such, actually chopping small tree's down, ect!!

These non paying people simply walked past the flimsy security, & easily tore down the weak fences & barricades that were set up to handle 'possibly' 100,000 people!! At least that's the # I kept hearing whilst we were there. Our band saw this tv footage that was taken by tv stations as far away as you can believe!

EVERYWHERE our caravan of 2 full equipment trucks & 3 cars stopped on our long drive home, we all saw footage of the scene, which was a sight i'll NEVER forget!

It was on the BIG 3 Network Nat'l Nightly news(!!)& the local tv stations & EVERY radio station. The nearby & other farther away food stores & gas stations were 'ravaged' by kids AND simply CLOSED b/c of the amount of people coming 'unprepared' for what was a 3 & 1/2 day affair, & buying every bit of food & drink!!

Cars parked 15-20 miles from the site, & as we learned throughout the 2 & 1/2 days we were there, many audients hiked as far as 30 miles to get there; MANY just parking closest which happened to be right along the interstate & other highways... Cars were just parked, as tho they were abandonded!! If you wouldn't have been able to see the PEOPLE at the 'site' from the stage or see the footage of those hiking to it from the helicopter's film footage, the scene would have conjured up images of 'deserted cities' as in the films, "Day of the Dead" or "The Day After"!! I'll also inform Dan of some the well known bands, that DID play but Dan didn't know of to mention.

Anyway. IF YOU INDEED ENJOY THE MUSIC OF "GG" YOU SIMPLY MUST BUY THIS DVD!! The dvd is treasure to ANY Prog/Rock fan, & I CAN ONLY HOPE THERE 'IS MORE' TO BE UNEARTHED, B/C THIS GREAT DVD WILL SIMPLY LEAVING YOU DYING TO SEE A FULL CONCERT!!

The material THAT IS available on this dvd, IS STAGGERING & WONDERFULL TO POSESS, HOWEVER ANYONE NOT OLD ENOUGH OR ANYONE THAT JUST MISSED SEING "THE GIANT" LIVE IN A FULL CONCERT WILL BE WAITING/HOPING(as I am)THAT 'THERE IS' AN ENTIRE CONCERT TO BE HAD!!!!!

I feel i've been blessed to play at major concerts during my 36 yrs of my career!!(Hell, even at 53 this isn't WORK...IT'S FUN!) Opening for a then nearly unknown "ZZ Top" as they had just released "Rio Grande Mud", at a place called the "North Twin DRIVE-IN" in STL, MO. in 72. A Drive-In of all places! This 1(as most of the big concerts of the late 60's thruout the late 80's were)was sponsered by "KSHE" Radio which began in 67 & was the only FM station here to play "that damned hippie music".

Kshe was very good to 2 bands I was in, as they arranged for us to record 2 quality demo's(Analog.Hey this is going back)& 1 was at our incredibly beautiful "Fox Theatre", to my knowledge one of only 2 that still exsist & anything that was broken down, worn out over the MANY yrs has been restored at huge cost by true craftsmen! I 'believe' Atlanta has the other Fox?

KSHE always sponsered us for big gig's & such. The 1 demo we made was going to be shopped at record companies, so they were powerful enough to get us the studio(I was floored when I learned of this, b/c I know Mo. & Il. & things musical there)which I DIDN'T EVEN KNOW EXSISTED. It was a state of the art studio on the(unbeknownst to me!)3rd fl or the "Fox Theatre"

I apologize for rambling so much. However I wanted to point out that a guy I know that used to work for KSHE is having a coffee table book published about the station. John Neiman. Gonna be a deluxe book.

Playing w/many internationally known acts, Leon Russell, Chuck Berry(1 vicious businessman!)Lee Michaels-Brownsville Station, many more like Nazerath & others listed on Dan's "Gentle Giant" Tour History, a must see for ANY "GG" Fan.

I apologize for seeming egotistical by what 'must' seem like tooting my own horn, as I really just wanted to tell every "GG" fan that was was SO LUCKY to have shared the same HUGE stage as the Giants of prog, & to meet a couple of them as well as so many other INCREDIBLY NICE & 'HUMBLE' hi quality musicians.(Well except Chuck B.!!:)I know I seem to be putting my history before

the REAL subject matter, the great "Gentle Giant" & this fabulous dvd, bu that was....& IS not my intention. Wether you belive it or not?? I seem to always inject some part of my life's

history into these reviews?! SORRY.

Fini. I just hope that anyone who has made it thru this diatribe

will go buy this fantastic dvd, & ALL THEIR ALBUMS, b/c wether you already know of "The Power & The Glory" that was/is GENTLE GIANT, or those JUST BEGINNING to appreciate the magnificence of their breakthrough seminal music, pls believe me, YOU WILL NOT BE DISAPOINTED!!

Tks to my mate in England(PAUL) who just visted me for 4 days in between alot more time spent w/his LADY friend in Portland! B/c I was NOT able to buy this impressive dvd WITH the bonus cd he got w/it by purchasing it early, & have just finished watching the entire dvd.(I still must listen to the NEW cd of HISTORIC "GG" music which will withstand the test of tim forever!

Thank You to the members of "GG" for your brilliance, & PLEASE CONSIDER A TOUR AGAIN!! Thanks to all of you who read ALL my babeling, I know it was a chore. Steven "Cannons" Munari

Whoops;-/ My friend Paul I spoke of? Well check this 1 out. He & his older brother Chris grew up directly across the street from the FINE guitarist Jakko Jakszyk who has played w/MANY fine British musicians & NOW plays w/the ex members of "King Crimson"

the incredible "21st Century Schizoid Band"(just type that in with [...] & com. & you learn of a incredible band doing what Robert Fripp NEVER would & that is to revisit 'Hallowed Ground" by performing "KC" music from their 1st album(IN The Court...)through their 4th, "Islands" as well as playing SOME new material! They have released a few live cd's of this incredible music AND also a live performance ON A DVD FROM JAPAN!!

So, Jakko & Chris took a very young Paul to his very 1st concert

& believe it or not that 1st concert(EVER)was to hear a LIVE "GENTLE GIANT"!! Wow. Thanks for your time!
top_rant.title: I was lucky enuff to play on the same stage w/"GG" in 1973!! LONG 1

top_rant.text:
Well, I started with The Unguided's 2011 debut entitled [[ASIN:B005VMHB7M Hell Frost]] (see my review) and have worked my way back through the history of the people in both bands (for those who don't know, Richard, Roger, and Roland were in this band and then left and eventually created The Unguided). I listened to [[ASIN:B001BMWNME Love & Other Disasters]] (and reviewed it) and it was an amazing album. This album is more of their great mix of melodic death metal and metalcore, but it's not as well done as the other two. It came before the other 2, however it's like a mix of them; The Unguided's Hell Frost is much heavier than S.S.'s Love... and this one is in the middle. However, it doesn't have the diversity of either, namely Love and Other Disasters. This album follows a pattern on nearly every song (harsh verses and clean choruses) and it's only broken by Enclave, which is an amazing song! The band is still doing well on this album though. Roger and Robin provide some excellent guitar riffs, John gives us some solid beats, Karin gives some good bass lines, and Richard & Roland show up every other metalcore band how vocals are supposed to be done. The DVD that is included with this Tour Edition is cool. It has some music videos and other stuff...but the main reason I got the DVD was because a. I wanted the bonus track on the album and b. There's a concert on the DVD. The concert is really good!

The music:

1. Aftermath- It starts off quietly and then the riffs kick us into something truly epic! The keys are integrated as a melody perfectly over some good guitar-ing! The guitar melodies underneath our vocalists are nothing short of SPECTACULAR! Roland is a GODLY vocalist!!! His voice is what Heaven sounds like when it's screaming down to you! The singing in this song is passionate and very well done! Richard's screams are good. They get better with time (and this is only the 2nd major release he did. So not as good as later albums). The thing that I crave in the middle of the song would be a solo. That would've been good. It just feels a little empty. But it's a short emptiness and Roland comes back to sing again for us! The drum part on this is intense and sounds like a boat load of fun to play! I'll try it later! Good album opener!

2. Blue Eyed Friend- This starts off with a short drum fill and the guitars playing some chords. However, this one doesn't have the intensity of the opener and it falls short to my ears. Of course it isn't a bad song, but it doesn't impress me. It isn't something that gets me moving and motivated.

3. Psychic Suicide- This starts off with a pretty freakin' awesome guitar part and then it just pounds you with Richard and Roland's dual screams! Then they play some awesome guitar leads underneath Roland's epic voice! The chorus is very good! I love singing along to it and it's easily a Sonic Syndicate classic! The drums are just thrashing through everything! The bass lines aren't very audible, but at times, they can be heard and they are top notch! This is one that will get you headbanging like crazy and the middle of this song doesn't feel empty cause the guitars are playing a melody with some keys doing some stuff over them. The guitars on the track are particularly noteworthy with their harmonizing underneath Roland's voice during the pre-chorus and it really gives it that melodic death metal sound! This is an amazing song and I particularly like the live version! Great stuff!

4. Double Agent 616- This song just starts quickly! It has a promising start, but it doesn't do much for me either. I was waiting for it to grow on me, but it never did. It just doesn't have that energy level. The guitars are good in some parts, but that's about all I can say about this one.

5. Enclave- This is a masterpiece. This is one of Sonic Syndicate's best songs! They will never beat it, especially now that Roland left. Roland is the only vocalist on this one and it is a melancholy ballad. The music, while not advanced is PERFECT! The guitar part is so relaxing and sad and Roland sings beautifully over them! The drums do have one fast part in the middle and the song is truly great! This isn't your typical radio garbage ballad. This isn't even a typical ballad. This is MUSICAL PERFECTION! If you don't see the beauty in this song, you honestly don't know what true music is. Do you hear any other ballads that remotely resemble this? I sure haven't. Sonic Syndicate made a ballad that isn't smothered by cheesy lyrics, isn't ruined by sappiness, and isn't something we've heard before. Everything is in perfect balance and this is easily one of the strongest moments of the album as well as of Sonic Syndicate's career. Musicianship doesn't get much better than this. Phenomenal song! Absolutely phenomenal!

6. Denied- This song picks things back up rather quickly! This is one of the few exceptions to their precise formula as well. Roland has much more presence on this song as well. Richard only has half of each verse and one line in the chorus. Roland takes care of the rest! This song definitely has the best instrumental track of all the fast paced songs on this album! The guitar leads RIVAL OLD In Flames! They are so well done! The drumming part is nice and varied and has lots of pounding! The backing keys add some very epic atmospheric lines and the guitar melody in the instrumental break is AWESOME! Roland's voice on this one is GREAT! This song is great! This song is a sign of what's to come on the next album (Love and Other Disasters) where all the rockers have the level of instrumental prowess as this one! GREAT TRACK!!!

7. Callous- This one has an epic intro key part over the band doing some typical riffing and the verse is aggressive as usual. Richard shines on this song too. I like his vocals on this one a lot! Roland of course is spectacular and the instruments are doing their things. The guitar riffing is very intense, very good, and very heavy. The chorus of this one is good too. The middle instrumental section is actually very distinct and has a very memorable melody between the two guitar parts. I enjoy the middle part of this song a lot! This isn't one of the best songs, but it's still a solid song! Very enjoyable song! Great job!

8. Only Inhuman- The title track! It starts off with some good key leads and then the guitars assault you quickly and mercilessly! I really enjoy this one. The vocals really stand out on this one! The instrumental part is BRUTAL! The chorus of this one is one of my favorite choruses of the album! This a very good chorus with a great set of lyrics to sing along to! The guitar are absolutely killer and the drumming keeps a steady beat! The middle part is very headband-tempting! I can't resist :p Then Richard comes back in and it's more intense! Then it gets soft and Roland sings soft and it's really AWESOME! I love that little soft bit! It brings out more emotion! The chorus is already emotional, but that soft part is just GREAT! It's brilliant! Great song!

9. All About Us- This is a cover. I don't know the original artist off the top of my head, but I've heard the original, and Sonic Syndicate actually does the song justice. The original is done by a pop singer with ear-piercingly high vocals. Roland definitely sings this with more passion and more dedication than she did. Richard's screams definitely help get rid of the pop influence of the original as well. The riffing is limited due to it not be written by the band. It's a good cover and a good track. But it's definitely not as strong as their songs on the album. This is one that you can still rock out to though! Solid song!

10. Unknown Entity- This one starts off pretty quickly. By this point, I wish the keys would step aside and let one guitar to some lead parts, but it's still acceptable. This song kicks a**!!! I love the vocal melodies, the guitar riffing in the verses and I really enjoy Roland's vocal emotion! This song definitely is one to headbang to. Roger and Robin's guitar work is still great as ever and the drums are still varied, but not as much as before. The live version of this is also great. There is a soft part in this one too and I really like this in here too! Roland makes it good! This is one that I love to sing along to as well as bang my head along with! This is quite a good song!

11. Flashback- This song is much slower than the others. It's a slower melodic death tune similar to what we got on Eden Fire (the previous album). Honestly I'm not a big fan of this song. It's ok. Roland sings well of course and the music's good, but it doesn't do much for me. Not as good as the other songs.

Bonus Track!

12. Freelancer- I HIGHLY ENJOY THIS SONG!!! IT IS AMAZING! The melodies are really good and the riffs are superb! The dual vocals really go for the jugular and the harmonies on this one are excellent! The chorus is very good and I would love to hear this song performed live! This song is REALLY worth getting! This song is ESSENTIAL! If you don't have it, you are sorely missing out on something spectacular! The intensity of this song is just BRUTAL! The In Flames influence is definitely heard, however, it's much better in execution! Jesper Strömblad will hear this song and shake his head and wonder why he didn't write it! AWESOME closing song!

Verdict! If you already have this album, download the bonus song. It's VERY GOOD! If you don't own this album, do get this version. The concert, while not high end, is enjoyable and the extra song is worth having! I will say though, that I like the songs on this one, but I definitely enjoyed Love and Other Disasters more than this one. Sonic Syndicate is still around, but they aren't making music like this anymore, that's for sure. So long live The Unguided. If you like S.S., check out Hell Frost by The Unguided.
top_rant.title: Great Stuff!

top_rant.text:
Yes, this album is great progressive metal and yes it's better than whatever progressive metal you have heard before! This band is its own. You really can't compare their music to anyone else's because Evergrey is truly unique! There is no other band in the world that sounds like this. No other band makes music so complex, yet so emotional. No other band has 5 guys playing music this heavy that is this beautiful! Tom S. Englund is one of the greatest vocalists in metal today. He is also one heck of a guitar player! Henrik Danhage is the other guitar player on this album and these two make an EXTRAORDINARY duo! This band is from Sweden and they manage to capture that Gothenburg Melodic Death Metal Guitar tone, but they add a new flavor and flair to it. I'd compare these two to Jesper and Björn of In Flames, but Tom and Henrik are on a whole other level of playing. That's right, Jesper and Björn can't match Evergrey! And to be honest, no guitar duo is on their level! Not Li/Totman of DragonForce, not Friedman/Mustaine of Megadeth, not even Smith/Murray of Iron Maiden match this level! The closest guitar duo in my mind is Sjunnesson/Johansson of The Unguided and they're still way over on left field, not to mention that they just made their first album in 2011 (10 years after the release of this Evergrey album!)!. Michael Håkansson is an awesome bassist when he can be heard. Sven Karlsson's keys are just unheard of and Patrick Carlsson's drumming is played aggressively but also tastefully. Yes, I may seem like I'm over-exaggerating how good these guitar players are and I'm just biased about their music because I'm such a fan of this band. If you don't believe me, just listen to their music! Hear how good these guys really are for yourself if you don't believe me!

The Music
-This is a concept album about a guy having illusions of being abducted by Aliens and trying to figure out if they're real or fake memories.

1. The Masterplan- This starts off with the opening tape recording of our protagonist and then Sven's key line comes in and then the rest of the band starts riffing. At first they sound similar to say Symphony X and then Englund and Danhage do some trademark Evergrey melodies full of a passion no other duo can match. Then Tom begins to sing and takes the song to an even higher level!!! The chorus in this one is killer! It's great! It sounds very full with the choir backing Tom up! The verses groove along and the guitar melodies and harmonies are really something you don't hear from metal. You may hear metal bands with singers that have emotion and power, but never a FULL BAND where the emotion comes through all 5 instruments! The instrumental section is amazing and the solo(s) are just BEYOND GODLY! This song starts off the album on the right foot! This song is a classic Evergrey song and I love it! The solo at the end during the last chorus is very skilled and tastefully executed! AWESOME SONG!

2. Rulers of the Mind- This song is still Evergrey and it's still entertaining, but this is one of two songs on this album that fell flat for me. It's not a bad song at all, but it's not one that I'd jam out to on a daily basis. It's got the makings of a great Evergrey song, but it isn't as impressive as the other songs! It does have a nice instrumental section though!

3. Watching the Skies- This song is a similar story to the previous track. It's a great, typical Evergrey song, but it pales in comparison to the rest of this album! It isn't a bad song by any means, but compared to The Masterplan and the songs after this one, this song isn't as good :p

4. State of Paralysis- Sven really gets a chance to show off his skills as a piano player on this one. This is all Sven and Tom and it is a very beautiful song! It's a prelude to the following song, but this song is more than just that! It's the sound of pure, raw beauty! Tom conveys the lyrics with such power! And Sven's key part is beautiful! It really is something special! Good song!

5. The Encounter- This song bursts off the last note of the previous track and it's an intense rocker! In terms of the song's style, think Symphony X's The Accolade, but again, the way Evergrey makes their music is on a different caliber than other Prog bands! This one is filled with some very melodic and technically advanced guitar runs! Yet it still has the sound of emotion! The guitar parts on this song are top notch! Harmonizing has never sounded this Godly! The drumming here is very good! It drives the song at a steady pace! The guitar solo is oh so passionate! It's played with so much gusto and fire! Towards the end of the song, the band turns it up a notch with some strong rhythm beats and some neo-classical guitar parts that really sound tasty! This track is AWESOME!

6. Mark of the Triangle- This song and Misled are my favorite songs of the album! This song is full of great things musically! The piano plays some very nice parts in this song! Englund's vocals on this one are just OUTSTANDING! The guitars are doing some very cool things and the drums are keeping time in a unique way throughout! Michael's bass part here is VERY NEAT! Very original! The riff on the second verse is nothing short of SPECTACULAR! Their guitar melodies are endless! There are so many within just this ONE SONG!!! I love the progressions this song goes through! This song will have you head banging, it will have you singing/humming along, and air guitaring along! The solo on this one, while 80's influenced, is my favorite solo on this album!!! This song is my favorite of the album for a reason; every member has a chance to shine on this one! The music of it though has what most metal is missing in the 21st century: Power. Not power as in maxing everything out, but power as in it has purpose and emotion to retain replay value. It's good for those who want something to sing along to, those who want to head bang, and those who want good, inspired lyrics. AWESOME SONG!!!

7. Dark Waters- This song is probably the darkest one on the album. It features the band playing some aggressive and emotional riffs and melodies! The choir on this one adds an eerie quality to the already dark music! The keys and the bass really make this song have that deep tone. The guitar melodies and harmonies, however keep a sense of hope and lightness in the music! Tom's vocals keep the listener feeling something throughout the song! He delivers a very powerful punch! The way this song is constructed is pure genius! The Gothenburg sound is in this one quite a bit! The drumming here is nothing short of amazing and the guitar riffs are the same! The keys are very complex but mostly atmospheric and doing some leads for some parts! Englund and Danhage may not fill each song to the maximum with leads, but they do fill up a good 85% of the otherwise empty space with some riffs and melodies that would make Jesper Strömblad jealous! Good song!

8. Different Worlds- This is basically the album's ballad. It is filled with keys, vocals and at the right times, some high quality guitar work! This song is very sparse at the beginning and Tom carries most of the song! The acoustic guitar parts are kinda in the background of the mix, but they are still good. Sven is a highly underrated keyboardist. The piano passages through this song are more impressive than half of the things Jordan Rudess has done with Dream Theater! My only beef with this song is that the tape recording of the protagonist of the story is kind of annoying in this one. But after the tape recording, we have a nice guitar melody come in and there's just a slight backing drum beat and bass part. But this song is driven by Sven when Tom isn't singing. This song is very strong, but the tape recording kind of hinders the middle part of it. But it's still a great song!

9. Misled- This is a great song! I love the work that Tom and Henrik put into their melodies on this song! The first verse is soft and the piano plays a cool melody under Englund's vocals and then the guitars and choir come in and the song speeds up with the best guitar melodies of the album! The melodies on here, while they only come in short doses are HEAVENLY DIVINE!!! In the second verse, the guitars play the melody the piano was playing in the first verse and it sounds BETTER and then they do their awesome melodies again!!! The progressive nature of this song is very obvious and the sheer awesomeness and quality of the music in the instrumental passage is UNMATCHED! The solo here is amazing and the drum beat under it sounds very good! The technical prowess of this band may not be as high as say Dream Theater, but Dream Theater certainly never made anything that has made me feel emotion that's for sure! This is a FANTASTIC FINALE to a classic Evergrey album!

Verdict: If you don't own this album and you like metal that has melody, you really need to buy this. The melodies in this are raining down from Heaven! If you like Progressive Music, you NEED this! I guarantee you that with 3 listens, you'll be begging for more Evergrey! The thing with this band is that they fit in with many different sub genres of Metal like Gothic Metal, Progressive Metal, Power Metal, and just Heavy Metal! However, you'll notice all of these different sounds flow together into something extraordinary and something worth listening to! If anyone has mixed these 4 subgenres together before, they probably don't do it nearly as well as Evergrey!

For the record, this is only the 2nd album I've heard from them...and even with as much praise as I've given it, I still find their 2008 effort, [[ASIN:B001CISHOA Torn]] to be even more impressive and powerful! But if you're just checking this band out for the first time, you can't go wrong with either, as you'll probably end up buying all their stuff sooner or later! :)
top_rant.title: The Greatest Progressive Metal You've Never Heard of!

top_rant.text:
...because it's Journey (one of the best rock bands of all time), it honestly isn't bad, but it's way below Journey's usual standards, and because I feel like rating it a 3 would be too harsh. Ok, so you can tell just from that first sentence and the title that this doesn't rank as a great Journey album. But it isn't bad. Contrary to belief, however, Trial by Fire is MUCH better than this album, so anyone that says otherwise hasn't really listened to TbF. Now, the lineup on this album is the very talented Steve Perry on vocals, who delivers the usual amazing performance. Neal Schon has some moments on here, but the absence of great leads and hooks from the guitar is BLATANT and is really what makes this album weak! This album is too stripped down from the rock that we got on Frontiers and even on Escape! Jonathan Cain is a good keyboardist, but he has WAY too much presence on this album. His key parts act as a lead and they make the music sound weaker because a guitar should have played those leads. That is the only official lineup on here. The studio musicians satisfy in some spots and drastically add to the weakness on others. Steve Smith is on 3 songs and that's good because I love his drumming, but sadly, 2 of those songs are so weak, that I don't even care that he's drumming on them! Bob Glaub does the bass on the tracks with Smith and he's not bad, but he's not Ross Valory (who is very missed on this album). Randy Jackson handles bass for the majority of the album and Larrie Londin does the rest of the drumming. Overall, the musicianship on this album is severely lacking when compared to ALL other albums by Journey (yes, even POST-Steve Perry albums have better musicianship than this). Overall, it's a very mixed bag.

The music:

1. Girl Can't Help It- This starts off the album on a good not. While it's not Journey's greatest hit, it's a decent hit, very mellow, nice song. It is mostly rock, but it has some elements of smooth jazz in it. This one actually has a good amount of Schon in it. I do appreciate that. However, Cain's keys have too much presence at times. The drums on this one don't annoy me. (The studio drummer, Larrie, sounds stiff on a lot of the songs and it bugs me). However, this actually sounds like a real musician playing the drums rather than a machine. It's a solid opener to this album. It's definitely a sing-a-long song. I will say that the solo towards the end is pretty well done and Perry sounds good through this song. Not bad. Pretty good actually!

2. Positive Touch- I always thought of Positive Touch as being a reference to the magical touch Steve Smith brings to Journey's drum tracks. This is the one track that is really good and has Smith drumming! It's a bouncy rocker and I quite enjoy it! It's got Cain doing a rock piano part and Schon playing some good bluesy leads and Steve Perry sounds SO GOOD on this song! This is the best performance of the album in my opinion. This and only one other song are absolute musts from this album! The solo on this song is KILLER! Schon definitely has the required presence on this song! There's also a saxophone solo in it that really adds a new flavor to the song as well as to Journey's catalog! I really enjoy this song! It's really one of their best songs and it's one of the 2 best songs that comes from this album! This song alone shows talent, musicianship, and inspiration. This is an absolute STANDOUT SONG!!!

3. Suzanne- Well...here's that drum machine sound that I really hate. Certain bands can pull off this sound, but Journey has always been better than that. They've never had to try to pull it off and here, they've slipped to just that. They just play a typical pop song with robotic drumming, lots of keys, and I'll admit, Schon has some presence in this song at times, but I think it was a waste. The drumming is SO UNINSPIRED and SO STIFF that it ruins it for me. As my favorite band of all time, I know they've made MUCH better than this! Plus Steve Smith is my favorite drummer and he is SURELY missed on this song (not to mention 3/4 of this album!) While Perry delivers good vocals, the rhythm section as well as the other aspects of the music are too unoriginal and too stereotypical 80's...Journey isn't you're stereotypical 80's sound...They are a great rock band, but not on this song.

4. Be Good to Yourself- Definitely the one single on here that sounds like the Journey of old (minus Steve Smith's superior drumming). While I admit the drumming on this one is more relaxed and actually fits the music, it's still not Smith. But in other aspects, Perry delivers a great vocal performance on this one! I love his voice on this song quite a bit! It sounds inspired! I like the energy he has for this one! Cain's keys don't overstay their welcome! They actually blend like they did on Frontiers! And Schon's guitar is godly yet again! The actual part is really good and the solo is probably the best solo of the album! It's probably the longest solo he has on the album if I'm not mistaking. I believe this song could've easily went well with the stuff on Frontiers, however, it saves this album from being worthless as the other singles it has are really worthless (Girl Can't Help It being the exception to that). Good rocker!!!

5. Once You Love Somebody- The opening guitar/bass riff sounds really good!!! But then Cain's keys really turn this into another stereotypical 80's song. Honestly, this is the WORST one on the album! I only hear a sellout band playing this song. I don't believe that Journey ever sold out before this album...but this album could be the one I label as sellout, mainly because of songs like this one! This one really does annoy me this much! I just don't like it in every aspect!

6. Happy to Give- This one sounds like something Perry didn't put on his solo album. I like the song, but not too much. If it was on his solo album, I'd probably say that this is a pretty good song! Maybe even solid! However, as a Journey song, it's sappy without the level of emotion and inspiration of previous ballads. Open Arms, Faithfully, Stay Awhile, Lights, etc. are all inspired and have real passion and emotion. Steve can make anything sound emotional and sell that. However, the genuine emotion that you hear from him on EVERY OTHER Journey album is absent on this album (with a few places having the emotion!). This song doesn't have the emotion. Sorry. I'm not feeling it.

7. Raised on Radio- This song...I can't decipher what the song is even about, but the music on this one is just AWESOME!!!! I wouldn't have minded the whole album to be in the direction that this song is in. This one, even though it still sounds more aimed to be played on the radio than their previous songs, even their previous HITS, it's not bad! It's one that has great musicianship, great key/guitar balance, and good vocals. The drums aren't too overly robotic, but they aren't as natural as I'd like. But I'll ignore the drumming on this one simply because the rest of the band is locked in and completely rockin'! They deliver a very solid rocker and I have no problem saying that this is the heaviest and hardest-rocking song of the album! Nice one!

8. I'll be Alright Without You- As a radio song, it's not bad at all. It has a nice beat and it's nice and mellow. As a Journey song though, I find it to be a little too light. Even a song like Send Her My Love has more edge because of the musicianship that gave it backbone and a pair, as well as the emotion. This song, while it has the emotion, suffers from not having enough musicianship. The guitarwork is noteworthy and respectable, but the rest of the song is good at times for me, but overall, it's meh.

9. It Could Have Been You- Ok. this song, along with Positive Touch, are MY FAVORITE songs off this album!!! Larrie Londin sounds like a real person on this song! His drum beat is PERFECT!!! There is musicianship on this song! There is a groove, there are guitar leads, there are soaring, inspired, and emotional vocals, and there are a healthy amount of keys! This song is a hidden gem! It Could Have Been a HIT!!! This one seriously is a stronger song than the preceding song by a long shot!!! Schon's solo and his lead part on this song is just awesome! This song is something that should be on their greatest hits compilations and should get more credit. However it isn't to be. Upon the first 2 listens to this song, you will be humming it in you head, you will air-guitaring to it, you will be singing along to it, you be jamming along to it! It is such an underrated song and such a hidden gem that I honestly want to go back in time and make them release it as a single! I say again, It Could Have Been a Hit! Or more like It SHOULD Have Been a Hit! GREAT song!!!

10. The Eyes of a Woman- This song is a mellow song akin to the style of Send Her My Love, but it's not as well done. Steve Smith is even playing the drums on this one, but it sounds too much like the band was trying to repeat the success of Send Her... and it just backfired. It, of douse, isn't a bad song, but I just find it to be a bit too much in that familiar territory to be awesome. This song doesn't do anything for me.

11. Why Can't This Night Go on Forever- This is a ballad, very similar to previous monster hits like Open Arms, Faithfully, and even Still They Ride. I won't really complain about the musicianship or emotion on the song...why? Because it's THERE!!!! Then what's wrong with it you ask? Same as the previous song, this sounds too similar to Faithfully. The guitar solo towards the end reminds me of Faithfully's famous guitar solo that was filled with fire and passion. This one sounds like a later re-attempt to copy it. Steve Smith's touch on ballads such as this are always a plus (aside from Journey's stuff, take a listen to the ballad, Heaven by Bryan Adams, that's Smith!). Honestly, the emotion and great things are here, they just stayed in territory that was too familiar!!!

Verdict: I am a huge Journey fan, and therefore, I own all of their studio albums. I enjoy their music, regardless of whether or not they have Steve Perry. This is the one album I've heard that just has a couple good songs and then a bunch of "so-so" songs. If you like Journey, odds are you'll either hate it entirely or you'll like certain songs. If you like 80's pop, you'll just LOVE IT!!! If you like Steve Perry, you already own it.

There are 3 categories of songs on here to my ear: Good, bad, and familiar. The Good ones are tracks 1, 2, 7, & 9. What they have in common is that they show Journey experimenting with CONFIDENCE!!! They OWN IT! The bad are tracks 3, 5, 6, & 8. These songs either just flat out suck, or show a Journey that is experimenting with either a) Steve Perry solo type stuff or b) without strength and gusto. The remaining tracks (4, 10, & 11) are familiar. They sound like Classic Journey. In the case of 4, it has a few new twists, but otherwise it's a good ol' classic Journey sound. 10 & 11 though are too familiar and are just kinda there.

All in all, it's worth the buy if you are more open-minded about music (I like music that has emotion and purpose, mainly rock and metal, though) or if you just like Journey and don't have it. People that are expecting another killer album like Escape or Frontiers might be disappointed. That said, if you seek something with the musical genius of Escape or Frontiers, try Trial by Fire. It's a hidden gem, much like the song It Could Have Been You.

Happy shopping!
top_rant.title: 3.5 Stars: Rounding Up...

RATED 5 STARS BY MOZART AUDIOPHILES #1 CHAMBER MUSIC CD MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO NO.10 [K.247 LODRON 1] & DIVERTIMENTO NO.17 [K.334 ROBINIG VON ROTENFELD].

CD REVIEWER NOTE TO AMAZON.COM READERS: I TYPE WITH LARGE CAP LETTERS TO READ WHAT I WRITE BECAUSE I AM A 100% DISABLED DECORATED VIETNAM VETERAN AND AM ALMOST BLIND WITH MACULAR DEGENERATION. I APOLOGIZE FOR ANY INCONVENIENCE LARGE CAPS MAY CAUSE YOU. ~PATRICK HILL, MOZART SCHOLAR & AUTHOR~

WOLFGANG AMADEUS MOZART [1756 - 1791]
*DIVERTIMENTO NO.10 IN F [K.247 LODRON 1]
*DIVERTIMENTO NO.17 IN D [K.334 ROBINIG VON ROTENFELD]
PERFORMED BY THE LEGENDARY VIENNA OCTET

THIS RARE OUT-OF-PRINT MOZART COLLECTORS CD IS FOUND IN MOST MOZARTEAN'S CD/MP3 COLLECTION!

THIS 1990 DIGITALLY REMASTERED CD WAS ORIGINALLY RECORDED BY THE CRITICALLY ACCLAIMED VIENNA OCTET IN 1956. ONLY THREE [3] CD RECORDINGS FROM THE VIENNA OCTET EXIST EVEN THOUGH THE VIENNA OCTET MADE OVER 30 BEST SELLING MOZART LP RECORDS IN THE 1950S AND 1960S.

ABOUT VIENNA OCTET OF THE VIENNA PHILHARMONIC
RATED AS ONE OF THE 20TH CENTURY'S GREATEST VIOLINISTS, WILLI BOSKOVSKY JOINED THE VIENNA ACADEMY OF MUSIC AT THE AGE OF 9. AT AGE 25, BOSKOVSKY BECAME CONCERTMASTER OF THE FAMED VIENNA PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA FROM 1936 TO 1979.

FORMED IN 1958 BY VIOLINIST/CONDUCTOR WILLI BOSKOVSKY, THE VIENNA OCTET WAS CREATED AS THE FIRST CHAMBER ENSEMBLE OF THE VIENNA PHILHARMONIC ORCHESTRA. FAMOUS THE THEIR HIGH STANDARDS, RAZOR-SHARP PRECISION, AND SRO PERFORMANCES, THE VIENNA OCTET'S ORIGINAL MEMBERS INCLUDED:

*WILLI BOSKOVSKY (1ST VIOLIN)
*PHILIPP MATHEIS (2ND VIOLIN)
*GUNTHER BREITENBACH (VIOLA)
*NIKOLAUS HÜBNER (VIOLONCELLO)
*JOHANN KRUMP (DOUBLE-BASS)
*ALFRED BOSKOVSKY (CLARINET)
*JOSEF VELEBA (HORN)
*RUDOLF HANZL (BASSOON)

MOZART COMPOSED HIS DIVERTIMENTO K.247, DIVERTIMENTO K.287, AND DIVERTIMENTO K.334 WITH MORE CHAMBER MUSIC SUBSTANCE AND HARMONIC RICHNESS BY INTEGRATING MORE CONCERTANTE ELEMENTS WITH THE VIOLIN CONCERTO INTO HIS NEWLY INVENTED DIVERTIMENTO AND SERENADE CLASSICAL MUSIC GENRE!

ABOUT MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO NO.10 IN F [K.247 LODRON 1]
COMPOSED IN 1776 AT SALZBURG WHEN MOZART WAS 2O YEARS OLD, DIVERTIMENTO NO.10 [K.247 LODRON 1] IS SCORED FOR 2 HORNS, 2 VIOLINS, VIOLA, AND DOUBLE BASS WITH SIX [6] MOVEMENTS. MOZART RECEIVED A COMMISSION FOR HIS DIVERTIMENTO K.247 FROM HIS PATRON COUNTESS ANTONIA LODRON, A PIANIST AND LEADING LIGHT OF SALZBURG'S CLASSICAL MUSIC LOVING ARISTOCRACY.

FOR HIS SAME PATRON COUNTESS ANTONIA LODRON, MOZART ALSO COMPOSED TWO OTHER MASTERPIECES HIS DIVERTIMENTO NO.15 IN B [K.287 LODRON 2] AND HIS PIANO CONCERTO NO.7 FOR 3 PIANOS IN F [K.242 LODRON] SO THAT HIS PATRON COUNTESS LODRON MIGHT PERFORM WITH HER TWO [2] DAUGHTERS BOTH OF WHOM WERE MOZART'S PUPILS.

MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO [K.247 LODRON 1] IS A GRAND SCALE VIOLIN CONCERTO THAT CONTAINS SIX [6] SUBSTANTIAL MOVEMENTS OF WORLD CLASS CHAMBER MUSIC. DIVERTIMENTO K.247'S SONATA ALLEGRO 1ST MOVEMENT DELIGHTS OUR EARS WITH A WEALTH OF INVENTION WHERE THE 1ST VIOLIN'S SOLO AND TUTTI PASSAGES HAVE A MORE PROMINENT ROLE AND LEAD THE DIALOGUE BETWEEN THE 2 HORNS, 2ND VIOLIN, VIOLA, AND DOUBLE BASS! THIS DOMINANCE OF DIVERTIMENTO K.247'S 1ST VIOLIN IS MAINTAINED THROUGHOUT THE REMAINING 5 MOVEMENTS. K.247'S ORNAMENTED ANDANTE 2ND MOVEMENT IS A SLOW RONDO WITH A MOOD OF DREAMY LONGING. IN DIVERTIMENTO K.247'S FIRST MINUET MENUETTO 3RD MOVEMENT AND SECOND MINUET MENUETTO TRIO 5TH MOVEMENT, ELEGANT DANCING THEMES AND DEEPLY EXPRESSIVE MELODIES WITH PIZZICATOS [PIZZICATO MEANS PLUCKING THE VIOLIN AND VIOLA STRINGS WITH FINGERS INSTEAD OF THE BOW TO CREATE A DIFFERENT SHORT PERCUSSIVE SOUND]! IN DIVERTIMENTO K.247'S ADAGIO 4TH MOVEMENT, MOZART COMPOSED A PURE STRING QUARTET WITHOUT THE HAUNTINGLY BEAUTIFUL 2 HORNS FOUND IN K.247'S OTHER 5 MOVEMENTS! IN DIVERTIMENTO K.247'S ROLLICKING CONTREDANSE [COUNTRY DANCE] FINALE 6TH MOVEMENT, MOZART COMPOSED A SCINTILLATING VIOLIN CONCERTO THAT EXHIBITS THE VERY LIFE AND SOUL OF CHAMBER MUSIC!

ABOUT MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO NO.17 IN D [K.334 ROBINIG VON ROTENFELD]
COMPOSED IN SALZBURG IN THE YEAR 1779, MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO NO.17 [K.334] IS SCORED FOR 2 HORNS, 2 VIOLINS, VIOLA, AND DOUBLE BASS. MOZART RECEIVED A COMMISSION FOR HIS DIVERTIMENTO K.334 FROM HIS PATRON AND FAMILY'S FRIENDS, THE ROBINIG VON ROTENFELDS, MEMBERS OF SALZBURG'S CLASSICAL MUSIC LOVING ARISTOCRACY. SPECIFICALLY, MOZART COMPOSED K.334 TO COMMEMORATE THE GRADUATION OF HIS CLOSE FRIEND SIGMUND ROBINIG FROM HIS LAW STUDIES AT THE UNIVERSITY OF SALZBURG IN 1780. SIGMUND ROBINIG WAS A SUPERB VIOLINIST AND PUPIL OF WOLFGANG MOZART. THUS, MOZART COMPOSED DIVERTIMENTO NO.17 K.334 WITH AN AMBITIOUS 1ST VIOLIN PART FOR SIGMUND ROBINIG TO PERORM.

CONSIDERED TO BE AMONG HIS MOST COMPLEX SOPHISTICATED RICHLY SCORED DIVERTIMENTI, MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTO K.334 IS A GRAND SCALE VIOLIN CONCERTO THAT CONTAINS SIX [6] SUBSTANTIAL MOVEMENTS OF WORLD CLASS CHAMBER MUSIC. WITH AN UNUSUAL LENGTH OF 45 MINUTES AND LONGER THAN ANY OTHER MOZART DIVERTIMENTI, DIVERTIMENTO K.334 CONTAINS SIGNIFICANTLY MORE STRUCTURE THAN MOZART'S TYPICAL DIVERTIMENTO. SCORED FOR A CHAMBER ENSEMBLE OF SIX INSTRUMENTS, DIVERTIMENTO K.334 IS A CHEERY CONTEMPLATIVE VIOLIN CONCERTO WITH EXPERTLY INTEGRATED VIOLINS, HORNS, CELLO, AND VIOLA THAT CREATED MOZART'S FINEST DIVERTIMENTO CHAMBER MUSIC. IN DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S SONATA ALLEGRO 1ST MOVEMENT, ALL SIX INSTRUMENTS CREATE AN EXCITING CHAMBER MUSIC DIALOGUE WHERE THE 1ST VIOLIN AND HORNS LEAD. IN DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S D MINOR ANDANTE 2ND MOVEMENT, ITS THEME IS ARTFULLY VARIED AND HEIGHTENED DURING THE 2ND MOVEMENT'S SIX VARIATIONS CALLED TEMA CON VARIAZIONI. DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S MENUETTO TRIO I 3RD MOVEMENT AND MENUETTO TRIO II 5TH MOVEMENT ARE TWO [2] MINUETS THAT FRAME K.334'S ADAGIO 4TH MOVEMENT WHICH IS AN ROMANTIC ENRAPTURED SERENADE WHERE THE FIRST VIOLIN LETS ITS CANTILENA [A SUSTAINED SMOOTH FLOWING MELODY] BLOSSOM TO THREE LINE OCTAVE HEIGHTS! DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S MENUETTO TRIO I 3RD MOVEMENT IS A FAMOUS DUET BETWEEN THE 1ST VIOLIN AND VIOLA WITH SOFT PIZZICATO ACCOMPANIMENT [PIZZICATO MEANS PLUCKING THE VIOLIN AND VIOLA STRINGS WITH FINGERS INSTEAD OF THE BOW TO CREATE A DIFFERENT SHORT PERCUSSIVE SOUND]! DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S MENUETTO TRIO II 5TH MOVEMENT IS CHARMING DANCE SERENADE THAT RESEMBLES A HIGHLY ENTERTAINTING GERMAN LANDLER. DIVERTIMENTO K.334'S CAPTIVATING RONDO 6TH MOVEMENT EXHIBITS MOZART'S INGENIOUS VIRTUOSO DEMANDS WITH A LIMITLESS FASCINATING THEMES, MEOLDIES, AND TONAL COLORS MADE ON THE 2 VIOLINISTS, 2 HORNS, CELLO, AND VIOLA!

ABOUT MOZART'S CHAMBER MUSIC DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES
IN THE 18TH CENTURY, ONE OF MOZART'S SPECIALTIES WAS COMPOSING FESTIVE ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC CALLED DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES TO BE PLAYED AS BACKGROUND MUSIC BY A SMALL CHAMBER MUSIC ENSEMBLE AT OUTDOOR SOCIAL EVENTS SUCH AS NAME DAYS, BIRTHDAYS, NEW YEAR'S FESTIVITIES, WEDDINGS, AND SCHOOL GRADUATION CELEBRATIONS WHERE PARTY GUESTS ENJOYING A DRINKS AND MEALS. THE WELL-TO-DO PATRON'S OF MOZART'S ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC [ALSO CALLED CHAMBER MUSIC] WERE USUALLY THE ARISTOCRACY IN MOZART'S NATIVE SALZBURG AND VIENNA. AT SOME ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC OCCASIONS, MOZART HIMSELF OFTEN PLAYED THE SOLO VIOLIN, VIOLA, AND PIANO PASSAGES OF HIS DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES.

ALTHOUGH MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES WERE FUN LIGHT HEARTED CHAMBER MUSIC UNLIKE HIS SERIOUS SYMPHONIES AND CONCERTOS WHICH WERE INTENDED TO BE PERFORMED IN A THEATRE OR CONCERT ROOM, MOZART'S ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC [ALSO CALLED CHAMBER MUSIC] OFFERS CLASSICAL MUSIC ENTHUSIASTS THE SAME BEAUTY, CHARM, AND UNIQUE QUALITY AS HIS MORE SERIOUS MATURE CLASSICAL WORKS.

RATED 5-STARS BY MOZART AUDIOPHILES WORLD'S #1 PERIOD INSTRUMENT CHAMBER MUSIC CD MOZART CLARINET QUINTET K.581 STADLER, OBOE QUARTET K.370, AND HORN QUINTET K.407 ACADEMY OF ANCIENT MUSIC CHAMBER ENSEMBLE!

CD REVIEWER NOTE TO AMAZON.COM READERS: I TYPE WITH LARGE CAP LETTERS TO READ WHAT I WRITE BECAUSE I AM A 100% DISABLED DECORATED VIETNAM VETERAN AND AM ALMOST BLIND WITH MACULAR DEGENERATION. I APOLOGIZE FOR ANY INCONVENIENCE LARGE CAPS MAY CAUSE YOU. ~PATRICK HILL, MOZART SCHOLAR & AUTHOR~

WOLFGANG AMADEUS MOZART [1756 - 1791]
*CLARINET QUINTET IN A [K.581 STADLER]
*OBOE QUARTET IN F [K.370]
*HORN QUINTET FOR STRINGS IN E [K.407]
PERFORMED BY THE LEGENDARY ACADEMY ANCIENT MUSIC CHAMBER ENSEMBLE

CD RELEASE DATE 1988
TOTAL PLAYING TIME 73:58 MINUTES

THIS OUT-OF-PRINT MOZART COLLECTOR'S CD IS FOUND IN MOST MOZARTEAN'S CD/MP3 COLLECTIONS!

THE ACADEMY OF ANCIENT MUSIC CHAMBER ENSEMBLE'S VIRTUOSO MUSICIANS FEATURED ON THIS CD ARE:
*ANTHONY PAY [CLARINET]
*STEVEN HAMMER [OBOE]
*MICHAEL THOMPSON [HORN]

CD TRACKLIST MISSING FROM ABOVE AMAZON.COM LISTING:
TRACK 1
CLARINET QUINTET IN A [K.581 STADLER]
I. ALLEGRO
II. LARGHETTO
III. MENUETTO
IV. ALLEGRETTO CON VARIAZIONI

TRACK 3
OBOE QUARTET IN F [K.370]

II. ADAGIO
III. RONDO ALLEGRO

TRACK 3
HORN QUINTET FOR STRINGS IN E [K.407]
I. ALLEGRO
II. ANDANTE
III. RONDO ALLEGRO

ABOUT MOZART'S CLARINET QUINTET IN A [K.581 STADLER]
COMPOSED IN 1789 FOR THE HIS FRIEND ANTON STADLER ONE OF THE 18TH CENTURY'S GREATEST CLARINETISTS OF VIENNA'S ROYAL COURT ORCHESTRA, MOZART'S CLARINET QUINTET K.581 IS SCORED FOUR [4] GLORIUS MOVEMENTS FOR 1 BASSETT CLARINET AND A STRING QUARTET OF 2 VIOLINS, 1 VIOLA AND 1 CELLO. MOZART'S CLARINET QUINTET IN A [K.581 STADLER] IS RENOWNED AS THE WORLD'S MOST BEAUTIFUL CHAMBER MUSIC FOR ITS BEAUTIFUL LYRICAL MELODIES! MOZART COMPOSED HIS CLARINET QUINTET K.581 WITH A WIDE VARIETY OF MOODS, DIVERSITY OF MUSICAL TEXTURES, AND SOPHISTICATED RICHNESS TO ENABLE THE CLARINETIST TO DISPLAY HIS VIRTUOSO TALENTS AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE.

ABOUT MOZART'S OBOE QUARTET IN F [K.370]
COMPOSED IN 1782 FOR THE HIS FRIEND FRIEDRICH RAMM WHO WAS ONE OF THE GREATEST OBOISTS OF THE LATE 18TH CENTURY FROM EUROPE'S FAMED MUNICH ORCHESTRA, MOZART'S K.370 IS SCORED FOR ONE OBOE, ONE VIOLIN, ONE VIOLA AND ONE CELLO. K.370 IS MOZART'S ONLY COMPLETED OBOE QUARTET AND IS RANKED BY MOZART ENTHUSISTS AMONG OF THE WORLD'S MOST BEAUTIFUL CHAMBER MUSIC!

MOZART SPENT THE WINTER OF 1780-1781 IN MUNICH, PUTTING THE FINISHING TOUCHES ON HIS NEW OPERA IDOMENEO IN PREPARATION FOR ITS PREMIERE ON JANUARY 29, 1781. WHILE THERE HE MET FRIEND FRIEDRICH RAMM, AN EXTRAORDINARY OBOIST FOR WHOM MOZART COMPOSED THE OBOE QUARTET FOR VIOLIN, VIOLA, & CELLO IN F [K.370] GIVING THE OBOE THE GREATEST POSSIBLE LEVEL OF PROMINENCE. THE IMPORTANCE OF THE OBOE PART IN K.370 DOES NOT MEAN THAT THE STRING PARTS ARE NEGLIGIBLE. ON THE CONTRARY, MOZART GIVES THEM A GREATER ROLE IN K.370'S DIALOGUE BETWEEN INSTRUMENTS THAN HE DOES IN HIS FLUTE QUARTETS MAKING THE K.370 RANK AMONG HIS BEST INTEGRATED CHAMBER MUSIC WORKS. MOZART'S OBOIST FRIEND FRIEDRICH RAMM'S ARTISTRY MUST HAVE BEEN EXCEPTIONAL JUDGING BY K.370'S OPENING SONATA 1ST ALLEGRO MOVEMENT WHERE THE OBOE IS CHARGED WITH MOST OF THE THEMATIC PRESENTATION ALTHOUGH THE VIOLIN AND VIOLA SHARE IN THE MUSICAL ARGUMENT THROUGHOUT THIS MOVEMENT. K.370'S SLOW 2ND ADAGIO MOVEMENT ILLUSTRATES THE WIDE RANGE OF EXPRESSION OF MOZART'S OBOE QUARTET BECAUSE IT IS IN THE KEY D MINOR, A KEY IN WHICH MOZART HIS DEPTH OF FEELINGS. K.370 CLOSES WITH A CHEERFUL SPIRITED 3RD RONDO MOVEMENT IN WHICH CONTRASTING EPISODES BETWEEN INSTRUMENTS. WITH AN UNUSUAL, THIRTEEN-MEASURE SEGMENT IN WHICH THE OBOE PLAYS IN 4/4 METER WHILE THE STRINGS CONTINUE IN 6/8 NOT NOTICING THAT ONE OF THEIR NUMBER HAS GONE ASTRAY.

ABOUT MOZART'S HORN QUINTET FOR STRINGS IN E [K.407]
COMPOSED IN 1782 FOR THE HIS FRIEND JOSEPH LEUTGEB WHO WAS ONE OF THE GREATEST HORN PLAYERS OF THE LATE 18TH CENTURY FROM EUROPE'S FAMED MUNICH ORCHESTRA, MOZART'S K.407 IS SCORED FOR ONE HORN, ONE VIOLIN, TWO VIOLAS AND ONE CELLO. K.407 IS MOZART'S ONLY COMPLETED HORN QUINTET AND IS RANKED BY MOZART ENTHUSISTS AMONG OF THE WORLD'S MOST BEAUTIFUL CHAMBER MUSIC!

WOLFGANG MOZART'S HORN QUINTET FOR STRINGS IN E [K.407] IS THE WORK OF A COMPOSER DELIGHTING IN THE ART OF COMPOSITION. EACH OF K.407'S THREE MOVEMENTS IS A PARAGON OF BALANCE AND TONAL BEAUTY MANY CLASSCAL MUSIC CRITICS AND MOZART AUDIOPHILES HAVE PLACED MOZART'S K.407 ALONGSIDE HIS MASTERPIECE WIND CONCERTOS AND WITH GOOD REASON. THROUGHOUT K.407, THE HORN PROUDLY DISPLAYS ITS BRILLIANCE AGAINST THE ACCOMPANIMENT IN THE STRINGS. K.407 1ST ALLEGRO MOVEMENT PROVIDING MOMENTS OF HUMOR IN WHICH THE STRINGS MIMIC AND ATTEMPT TO CONFUSE THE UNSUSPECTING HORNIST. FOR K.407, MOZART CHOSE A STRING COMPLEMENT THAT INCLUDES TWO VIOLAS INSTEAD OF THE TRADITIONAL DOUBLE VIOLINS. THE RESULT IS AN ENSEMBLE SOUND OF GREATER WARMTH, COMPLEMENTING THE MELLIFLUOUS TONES OF THE HORN. THIS CHOICE OF INSTRUMENTATION WORKS TO GREAT EFFECT IN K.407'S 2ND ANDANTE MOVEMENT WHERE THE HORN TAKES UP A LOVE DUET BETWEEN THE HORN AND VIOLIN. K.407'S 3RD RONDO MOVEMENT FINALE BRINGS THE WORK TO AN APPROPRIATELY BOLD AND SPARKLING CLOSE WITH THE HORN AFFORDED ONE LAST CHANCE TO DISPLAY ITS VIRTUOSITY.

ABOUT MOZART'S CHAMBER MUSIC DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES
IN THE 18TH CENTURY, ONE OF MOZART'S SPECIALTIES WAS COMPOSING FESTIVE ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC CALLED DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES TO BE PLAYED AS BACKGROUND MUSIC BY A SMALL CHAMBER MUSIC ENSEMBLE AT OUTDOOR SOCIAL EVENTS SUCH AS NAME DAYS, BIRTHDAYS, NEW YEAR'S FESTIVITIES, WEDDINGS, AND SCHOOL GRADUATION CELEBRATIONS WHERE PARTY GUESTS ENJOYING A DRINKS AND MEALS. THE WELL-TO-DO PATRON'S OF MOZART'S ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC [ALSO CALLED CHAMBER MUSIC] WERE USUALLY THE ARISTOCRACY IN MOZART'S NATIVE SALZBURG AND VIENNA. AT SOME ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC OCCASIONS, MOZART HIMSELF OFTEN PLAYED THE SOLO VIOLIN, VIOLA, AND PIANO PASSAGES OF HIS DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES.

ALTHOUGH MOZART'S DIVERTIMENTI AND SERENADES WERE FUN LIGHT HEARTED CHAMBER MUSIC UNLIKE HIS SERIOUS SYMPHONIES AND CONCERTOS WHICH WERE INTENDED TO BE PERFORMED IN A THEATRE OR CONCERT ROOM, MOZART'S ENTERTAINMENT MUSIC [ALSO CALLED CHAMBER MUSIC] OFFERS CLASSICAL MUSIC ENTHUSIASTS THE SAME BEAUTY, CHARM, AND UNIQUE QUALITY AS HIS MORE SERIOUS MATURE CLASSICAL WORKS.
